project.title: Large scale screens of small molecules using multiple sources of human acute leukemia cells reveal novel targeting strategies
project.projectDescription: Useful targeted therapeutics for high risk subgroups of acute leukemia remain an unmet medical need. Here we report the results of a large-scale screening strategy that tested thousands of compounds targeting many signalling pathways for their ability to inhibit the survival and growth in vitro of human leukemic cells from multiple sources; i.e., patients’ samples, de novo generated human leukemia models, and established human leukemic cell lines. The responses of the cells from the de novo models exhibited similar responses to those of the patients’ samples both of which showed similar differences with the cell-line responses. Analysis of differences in subtype-specific therapeutic vulnerabilities made possible by the scale of this screen enabled the identification of a new specific modulators of apoptosis, shikonin, exemplifying its power to identify a relevant mechanism of action of small molecules with a complex polypharmacology. In summary, the results create a new platform for uncovering new therapeutic options for high-risk human leukemias, in addition to reinforcing the importance of the test sample choice for effective drug discovery.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Cells were resuspended in 5 volumes of 8 M Urea, 100 mm Tris-HCl pH 8.0. Samples were sonicated and spinned. Clear lysates were retrieved and protein concentration was quantified by Bradford Assay. 10 mg of protein lysates for each sample were reduced with TCEP 1mM (Thermofisher Scientific) and alkylated with chloroacetamide 55mM (Sigma Aldrich) for an hour at room temperature. Samples were diluted to 1M urea and 100 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0. Trypsin was added at enz/prot 1:20 (v/V0 and digestion was carried out overnight at 37oC. Samples were desalted on Sep PAK tC18 (500 mg) column (Waters). They were resuspended in 1.4 mL of IAP 1X (Cell Siganling provides the buffer in the kit at 10X). Samples were added to Ac-Lysine agarose beads and incubated 2 h at 4C under constant rotation. Beads were washed with 20mM Tris-HCL pH 7.6. Peptides were eluted twice with 100ul of 0.15% TFA in water with 2 min incubation. Samples were then dried down.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The data were processed using PEAKS X Pro (Bioinformatics Solutions, Waterloo, ON) and a Uniprot human database (20349 entries). Mass tolerances on precursor and fragment ions were 10 ppm and 0.01 Da, respectively. Fixed modification was carbamidomethyl (C). Variable selected posttranslational modifications were acetylation (N-ter), acetylation (K) oxidation (M), deamidation (NQ), phosphorylation (STY). Volcano plots were generated with Perseus 2.0.5.0.

references.referenceLine: Safa-Tahar-Henni S, Páez Martinez K, Gress V, Esparza N, Roques É, Bonnet-Magnaval F, Bilodeau M, Gagné V, Bresson E, Cardin S, El-Hachem N, Iasenza I, Alzial G, Boivin I, Nakamichi N, Soufflet AC, Mirela Pascariu C, Duchaine J, Mathien S, Bonneil É, Eppert K, Marinier A, Sauvageau G, Deblois G, Thibault P, Hébert J, Eaves CJ, Cellot S, Barabé F, Wilhelm BT. Comparative small molecule screening of primary human acute leukemias, engineered human leukemia and leukemia cell lines. Leukemia. 2025 39(1):29-41
